title: Samuel Jeffery Enjoys a Hearty Lunch at the Scenic Cruce Andino Transit Stop
description: Cruce Andino, Río Negro: Samuel Jeffery pauses for a quiet, refueling moment at a dining stop during the legendary lake crossing between Argentina and Chile. The frame captures a candid, energetic interaction with a crusty, toasted sandwich, highlighting the physical demands of traversing the rugged Andean wilderness. Soft, natural light streams through the adjacent windows, bathing the dining space in a warm, clean glow that contrasts with the overcast, dramatic mountain scenery waiting outside. The depth of field is tightly focused on the subject, rendering the background of the quiet, modern lodge in a gentle, atmospheric blur. His expression of genuine satisfaction speaks to the simple, essential comforts found amidst high-altitude transit. The composition emphasizes the intersection of modern travel convenience and the raw, untamed beauty of the surrounding Patagonian landscape. This scene serves as a testament to the importance of taking breaks during long-distance travel to fully appreciate the regional culinary traditions that fuel exploration. By documenting these unscripted intervals, the image offers a personal look at the human experience of navigating the complex, border-crossing geography of the Southern Cone. These moments of stillness are vital to understanding the pacing of travel through some of the world's most remote and awe-inspiring terrains. This image is a collaborative production by Samuel Jeffery and Audrey Bergner for Project 23.
